item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short, humorous, all in all pretty good. I'm glad we can still find some relatively funny articles in the main series.
However, the problem lies in the test log. A twenty megaton explosion seems like waaaaaay to much of an exaggeration. For reference, the Hiroshima and Nagasaki bombs had a yield of approximately 15, respectively 21 kilotons (0.015 and 0.021 megatons). Usually, this wouldn't be much of a problem, but this is just too over the top and it makes you look like you have no idea what you're talking about and/or didn't even bother to check your facts.
But yeah, other than that it's actually good

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it! In my opinion, quirky and snarky entries like this do have a place on the main page, theres already a ton of them. But it would also be cool to integrate it a bit more to the universe by asociating it somehow with Are We Cool Yet, this seems like their dream anomaly. Maybe as a tale, or an edit to the addendum about its retrieval that mentions that the Poi has been founf to be linked to AWCY. I could help you with either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm far too new to remember the days when S1 scips were being deleted, so no. I just brought this up as a shitpost in chat one day and a couple people said I should do it, so I did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was this inspired by a (long since deleted) old Series 100 SCP of glasses that threw back a sonic explosion when you put them on?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, some people really have trouble letting themselves be entertained! Maybe to avoid chafing so many butts you could somehow make this more obviously an anart object, maybe commissioned from AWCY or… Something…? Some people have said this belongs on the J page; well, maybe some anartist made them as a joke, right? Dunno, just throwing it out there.

These actually reminded me of "the glasses" from The Lost Room, you know, the ones that let you set shit on fire. I'm pretty sure "cool guys don't look at explosions" came later, but I like to imagine there's a connection.

… detonated with the estimated force of a twenty megaton bomb. All on-location parties were killed, but due to the volatile nature of SCP-1143 this test had been conducted in a remote location…

A very remote location! Great gravy!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just because a lot of people are using ridiculous titles doesn't make the titles any better. But the title is only a sidenote; the main problem is that the article is trite and unfunny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think most SCP entries now have names copied from, or referring to, various pop culture items. The Series IV list is especially guilty of that.

For example:
SCP-3022 - Hooked on a Feeling
SCP-3028 - Take On Me
SCP-3080 - The Power of Love
SCP-3089 - That Old Time Religion
SCP-3095 - Bird is the Word
etc.

It seems very bizarre, to me at least, to judge an SCP for its title, when we have such strange things out there as "OC DO NOT STEAL" and "STOPRIGHTTHERECRIMINALSCUM!!!".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please put full images in collapsibles — Accel

Ah, noted. My bad. — monorail

+ show image
- hide

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a glorified anomalous item, and not even a clever one at that. It should be a J article if anything. The tone is off in several places, and it looks like the whole article was written for the BADASS joke, which I find juvenile and not funny in the least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uncreative, title borrowed from a novelty song; this would be a lousy J and has no place on the main list…

item.title: Re: Yada Yada Yada This are a thing now, yeet
item.content:encoded: Dude, you finally posted this! I remember seeing the draft a long tiem ago, I'm glad after all this time it came out so well!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is just some good ol' fashioned simple goofiness and i love it. it's refreshing to see short, fun articles like this every now and again. well done ♥

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it makes you feel even better according to this 9173 person the Foundation is the stereotypical "evil action movie government/secret organization"

In terms of other badbois… so far there's 4-5 more that I've come up with that could show up in said tales.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes! Yes!

It’s here!

There is so much that’s right about this skip, but I gotta say my favorite part is that this PoI-9173 dude is just running around living a real-life action movie, and the Foundation is just picking up the bits and pieces. I’m just…so happy this exists and is canon (well, my canon). Now I’ll be waiting for somebody to drop these badboys into a tale. That’ll be the day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cool guys don't look at explosions…

Way to predict the title before I can change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cool guys don't look at explosions…
I personally like this, this is both silly and yet believable as an anomaly; and the execution is light hearted not trying to overthink itself.

Also, you have a stray SCP-X in the first addendum.

item.title: Yada Yada Yada This are a thing now, yeet
item.content:encoded:
Yea so this is a thing I did back at the beginning of doomcon. I know that there's more I could add to it but I either don't know what that is, am to lazy to do it/don't think I could do it right, really wanted a Series 2 spot (It's probably this one).

Thanks to Weryllium for telling me to go through with the original idea, Jazstar for giving me the last "S" variable (Had to get that BADASS acronym), CyrusFiredawn, multizig, and probably like 8 more people who's names I forgot cause my record-keeping skills are garbage for crit.

Uhh, yea that's it.
